title: Pure Simulation Core, Effect Adapters, Events, and Dependency Direction · Program Brief: Dependency Direction
summary: Entities read global input, clock, screen, sound, and random state; constructors load files; collisions mutate UI; tests require a window; and modules import each other cyclically. Predict player-visible behavior, state, units, timing, input, rendering, feedback, failure, accessibility, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the dependency direction increment in testable game core and adapter architecture. Learners progress through prediction, explici…
objectives: Direct dependencies from orchestration toward stable domain contracts and reject import cycles and hidden singletons.
Emit immutable collision, score, life, wave, cue, and transition records and consume them in explicit order.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-asteroids-python cumulative accessible real-time game portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python simulation models and fixed fixtures only.
Native pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers,
GPU behavior, frame pacing, accessibility playtests, packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class SimulationPolicy:
    fixed_step_seconds: float = 1 / 120
    max_frame_seconds: float = 0.1
    max_updates_per_frame: int = 8
    logical_width: int = 960
    logical_height: int = 540


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    revision: str
    seed: int
    input_trace_id: str
    runtime_id: str
    accessibility_profile: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Asteroids portfolio ready"

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Predict Dependency Direction · Theory Dependency Direction
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Asteroids team handles case 4199 during a low-vision laptop playtest at 125 percent scaling. Build deterministic Python evidence to separate deterministic domain state and systems from Pygame display, input, audio, clock, assets, and persistence adapters; retain player goal, revision, Python, pygame-ce, SDL, fixture, seed, input trace, viewport, and result identity; then replay the same input trace at 30, 60, and 144 presentation frames per second and compare simulation state. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Direct dependencies from orchestration toward stable domain contracts and reject import cycles and hidden singletons.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example Asteroids team handles case 4199 during a low-vision laptop playtest at 125 percent scaling. Build deterministic Python evidence to separate deterministic domain state and systems from Pygame display, input, audio, clock, assets, and persistence adapters; retain player goal, revision, Python, pygame-ce, SDL, fixture, seed, input trace, viewport, and result identity; then replay the same input trace at 30, 60, and 144 presentation frames per second and compare simulation state. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Direct dependencies from orchestration toward stable domain contracts and reject import cycles and hidden singletons. A defensible solution applies Dependency Direction, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to testable game core and adapter architecture.

content.code:
# Evidence: asteroids-worked-asteroids-architecture-pure-core-4
# Competency: asteroids-worked-asteroids-architecture-pure-core-4.
# Case 1655: a low-vision laptop playtest at 125 percent scaling.
# Operating constraint: test empty, zero-vector, seam, low-frame-rate, focus-loss, device-loss, missing-asset, corrupted-save, and rejected cases.
# Changed case: inject a 400 millisecond frame stall and prove the update budget prevents a spiral of death.
def worked_asteroids_architecture_pure_core_4(pure_systems: int = 8, effect_adapters: int = 4, hidden_globals: int = 0, dependency_cycles: int = 0, immutable_events: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked4 = "worked4-4dekrow-1655"
    assert evidence_variant_worked4
    evidence_axes_worked4 = (
        "playerworked4",
        "seed1655",
        "input4dekrow",
        "frameworked41655",
        "state1655worked4",
        "playtest4dekrow1655",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked4)) == 6
    if pure_systems < 4 or effect_adapters < 1:
        return False, pure_systems, "architecture-shape"
    if hidden_globals > 0:
        return False, hidden_globals, "hidden-state"
    if dependency_cycles > 0:
        return False, dependency_cycles, "dependency-cycle"
    if not immutable_events:
        return False, pure_systems, "mutable-events"
    assert pure_systems > effect_adapters
    return True, pure_systems, "testable-game-core"
content.caption: Runnable deterministic pure-Python Asteroids decision model; trace one accepted and one rejected frame, input, seam, collision, cue, save, package, or release case, then state which native pygame-ce, SDL, display, controller, audio, filesystem, GPU, accessibility playtest, installer, performance, recovery, or publication claim still needs controlled transfer evidence.

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Build Dependency Direction evidence · Theory Dependency Direction
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: asteroids-dependency-direction-17pmmk" that uses case 3562 to separate deterministic domain state and systems from Pygame display, input, audio, clock, assets, and persistence adapters. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not claim native pygame-ce, SDL, window, controller, mixer, filesystem, package, GPU, installer, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, released pygame-ce 2.5.7, fixed and headless SDL fixtures, real devices, licensed assets, accessibility playtests, profiling, clean installations, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

Evidence target: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Build Dependency Direction evidence · Theory Dependency Direction.

content.code:
# Evidence: asteroids-dependency-direction-17pmmk
# Competency: asteroids-dependency-direction.
# Case 3562: a keyboard-only community arcade night.
# Operating constraint: preserve explicit pixels, seconds, radians or degrees, frames, entity counts, collision units, and budgets.
# Changed case: run the packaged build with dummy video and audio drivers, missing optional sound, and a corrupted save.
def evidence_asteroids_dependency_direction_17pmmk(pure_systems: int = 8, effect_adapters: int = 4, hidden_globals: int = 0, dependency_cycles: int = 0, immutable_events: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_17pmmk = "17pmmk-kmmp71-3562"
    assert evidence_variant_17pmmk
    evidence_axes_17pmmk = (
        "player17pmmk",
        "seed3562",
        "inputkmmp71",
        "frame17pmmk3562",
        "state356217pmmk",
        "playtestkmmp713562",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_17pmmk)) == 6
    if pure_systems < 4 or effect_adapters < 1:
        return False, pure_systems, "architecture-shape"
    if hidden_globals > 0:
        return False, hidden_globals, "hidden-state"
    if dependency_cycles > 0:
        return False, dependency_cycles, "dependency-cycle"
    if not immutable_events:
        return False, pure_systems, "mutable-events"
    assert pure_systems > effect_adapters
    return True, pure_systems, "testable-game-core"

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Dependency Direction model · Theory Dependency Direction
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Dependency Direction: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory Asteroids team handles case 9961 during a headless continuous-integration replay. Build deterministic Python evidence to separate deterministic domain state and systems from Pygame display, input, audio, clock, assets, and persistence adapters; keep browser work pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of native display, device, audio, file, package, and operating-system effects; then lose window focus while thrust and fire are held, then prove both actions release safely. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Direct dependencies from orchestration toward stable domain contracts and reject import cycles and hidden singletons.

Evidence target: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Dependency Direction model · Theory Dependency Direction.

content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Direct dependencies from orchestration toward stable domain contracts and reject import cycles and hidden singletons.
